affected_services.details: Startup failure (npm ERR! ×54, ECONNREFUSED 172.18.0.3:80 ×413), up=0 for ~25% of scrapes, 5xx errors (max=2.919 errors/s at 15:59:09 UTC), memory spike (max=507.6MB at 16:20:54 UTC), CPU spike (4.0× avg at 16:15:24 UTC)
DNS resolution failures (dial tcp: lookup front-end ... i/o timeout ×2236, 16:06:24–16:25:32 UTC)
CPU throttling spike (12.4× avg at 15:59:54 UTC), CPU usage spike (0.01174 cores at 16:00:09 UTC)
CPU throttling spike (26.6× avg at 15:46:39 UTC), CPU usage spike (0.01321 cores at 15:46:39 UTC)
CPU throttling spike (64.5× avg at 15:43:09 UTC), CPU usage spike (0.01836 cores at 15:43:24 UTC)

root_cause.confidence_rationale: Two independent signals (logs and metrics) confirm DNS resolution failures and front-end startup failure. The causal chain is fully evidenced, and alternatives (memory leak, infrastructure throttling) are ruled out by lack of supporting evidence.
root_cause.summary: Docker DNS misconfiguration (127.0.0.11:53) and front-end startup failure (npm ERR!) caused a cascading outage, preventing the front-end service from handling traffic.

causal_chain.event: Startup failure due to npm ERR! and ECONNREFUSED 172.18.0.3:80
causal_chain.mechanism: front-end failed to initialize, preventing it from binding to port 8079
causal_chain.evidence: front-end logs: npm ERR! Failed at start script (×54), ECONNREFUSED 172.18.0.3:80 (×413)

causal_chain.mechanism: Docker DNS (127.0.0.11:53) timeout caused edge-router to drop requests
causal_chain.evidence: edge-router logs: dial tcp: lookup front-end ... i/o timeout (×2236)

causal_chain.mechanism: No healthy front-end to handle requests, causing 500 responses
Recovery attempts or traffic surge after partial restoration

contributing_factors: Missing logs for front-end pre-anomaly, preventing confirmation of initial health
No circuit breakers in edge-router, amplifying DNS retry storms
CPU throttling in downstream services due to untuned resource limits
alternatives_considered: Memory leak ruled out: No OOM logs, memory spike occurred after 5xx errors
Infrastructure throttling ruled out: CPU throttling spikes occurred after front-end was down
Network partition ruled out: No evidence of broader network issues (network_drops=0 for all services)

evidence.description: edge-router: dial tcp: lookup front-end on 127.0.0.11:53: read udp ... i/o timeout (×2236, 16:06:24–16:25:32 UTC); front-end: npm ERR! Failed at start script (×54), ECONNREFUSED 172.18.0.3:80 (×413)
job=frontend, up=0 (avg=0.74627); status_code=500 (max=2.919 errors/s at 15:59:09 UTC); container_memory (max=507,596,800 bytes at 16:20:54 UTC)

recommendations.action: Fix front-end startup script to resolve npm ERR! and ECONNREFUSED errors
recommendations.rationale: Prevents front-end from failing to start, addressing the primary root cause

recommendations.action: Validate Docker DNS configuration (127.0.0.11:53) and ensure front-end is resolvable
recommendations.rationale: Resolves DNS resolution failures in edge-router

recommendations.action: Add circuit breakers to edge-router to limit DNS retry storms
recommendations.rationale: Prevents amplification of failures during outages

recommendations.action: Increase resource limits for front-end, carts, orders, and shipping
recommendations.rationale: Mitigates CPU throttling and memory spikes during recovery

recommendations.action: Implement pre-anomaly logging for front-end to capture startup and runtime events

impact: Complete user-facing outage: front-end unavailability prevented all request processing, returning 5xx errors (2.919 errors/s) during peak traffic (7.8× avg requests). Downstream services (carts, orders, shipping) received no traffic but experienced CPU throttling due to untuned resource limits.
